basics.name: Lee E. Mullins
basics.label: Data Analyst & AI Specialist
basics.summary: Data analyst and healthcare AI specialist with a background in bioinformatics, MRI/QC workflows, and enterprise CRM tooling

work.name: Emory University
work.summary: Working on bioinformatics and MRI data analysis tools.
highlights: Developed Docker-based MRI QC pipelines (BIDS-compliant, PHI-scrubbing)
Built web-based QC tool for structural scans (NiBabies, MRIQC, Monaifbs)
Managed data harmonization across MRI, EHR, and genomics datasets
Led NIH/NDA submissions, IRB protocol support, and junior RA training
Streamlined internal data systems and server environments for reproducibility

projects.name: Tumor Cell Type Prediction App
projects.summary: 3D spatial transcriptomics classifier for tumor heterogeneity with 94% accuracy.
highlights: React frontend, AWS Lambda backend, DynamoDB for results storage
Maps Leading Edge vs Tumor Core in cancer tissue samples

projects.name: BIDS MRI QC Toolkit
projects.summary: CLI + web interface for fMRI and structural MRI QC.
highlights: Includes slice detection, bias correction, skull stripping, and HTML reports
Fully containerized via Docker and hosted on AWS

projects.name: Socratic AI Writing Coach
projects.summary: Chrome extension offering structured feedback inside Google Docs.
highlights: Uses AWS-hosted LLM (DeepSeek) for inference based on age + genre
Encourages critical thinking, never rewrites student work

projects.name: Salesforce AgentForce Hackathon
projects.summary: Custom data cleanup agent with Salesforce Admin partner.
highlights: Used Salesforce CLI, Metadata API, REST API, and Trailhead training
Performs deduplication, standardization, and field completion
Currently adding UI dashboard and multi-object support
